excerpt.rendered: Personal website on a decoupled stack: Next.js frontend, headless WordPress CMS. Built for resilient content delivery, CMS-driven layout, and independent deployment of each tier — engineered for performance, content flexibility, and long-term maintainability.

project_content.challenge:
Building a personal website that genuinely demonstrates full-stack capability means going beyond a static site generator. The goal was a platform that could handle several real-world requirements simultaneously:

  • Showcase portfolio work with rich, CMS-managed content — not hardcoded data
  • Load fast enough to score well on Core Web Vitals
  • Support publishing blog posts and project updates without touching code
  • Survive a CMS outage without the frontend going blank
  • Scale cleanly as the portfolio grows over time

Choosing a headless WordPress architecture introduced a set of concrete backend engineering problems that most tutorials skip entirely:

Content Model Drift

WordPress’s default post/page model isn’t expressive enough for a structured portfolio. I needed typed custom post types for projects, skills, hobbies, books, and tech entries — and those field definitions had to stay consistent across local, staging, and production. ACF field groups configured through the GUI tend to drift: fields added on production get forgotten on staging, lost during migrations, or silently mis-mapped after a plugin update.

CORS & Local Development Reliability

Headless setups are notorious for “works on my machine” bugs. CORS headers misconfigured for a single origin, preflight OPTIONS requests that fail silently, or the local Next.js frontend accidentally pointing at the production CMS — any of these can cost hours of debugging. Getting this right required a deliberate, configurable CORS implementation with debug visibility, not a wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

Frontend Redirect Safety

WordPress should not serve public-facing pages in a headless setup. But a naive blanket redirect breaks the REST API, the GraphQL endpoint, wp-admin access, and static asset loading. The redirect logic needed surgical precision: forward regular visitors to Next.js while leaving /wp-json/, /graphql, /wp-admin/, /wp-login.php, and all asset paths completely untouched.

Resilience Against CMS Downtime

A personal website that goes dark whenever the CMS has a maintenance window isn’t production-quality. The frontend needed a multi-tier fallback strategy to serve meaningful content independently of WordPress availability.
project_content.solution:
The solution is a two-layer architecture: a custom headless WordPress theme that acts as a structured content API, and a Next.js 16 frontend built for performance and CMS resilience.

Custom Headless WordPress Theme

Rather than bolting headless functionality onto an existing theme, I built a purpose-designed backend module system from scratch. Responsibilities are cleanly separated across modules in inc/:

  • Centralized configuration via HeadlessProConfig — frontend URL, allowed CORS origins, and redirect modes are wp-admin configurable, with PHP constants that override settings in production (“constants win” pattern for security)
  • Predictable CORS via HeadlessProCORS — correctly handles preflight OPTIONS requests, allows only configured origins, and supports debug headers to inspect CORS behavior without guesswork
  • Smart frontend redirect — regular visitors are forwarded to Next.js, but /wp-json/, /graphql, /wp-admin/, /wp-login.php, and all asset paths are explicitly excluded to keep APIs and admin access stable
  • Custom Post Types via REST + GraphQL — skill, hobby, project, tech, and book CPTs expose a clean, typed content schema to the frontend
  • ACF as code — all Advanced Custom Fields groups are defined in PHP under inc/acf-fields/, versioned in git, and identical across every environment — no migration surprises, no field drift
  • Headless Pro admin dashboard — a custom wp-admin panel surfaces REST and GraphQL health checks, CORS and redirect settings, environment diagnostics, and copy-paste curl commands for rapid API debugging without leaving the browser

Next.js 16 Frontend

The frontend uses Next.js App Router with TypeScript and SCSS, built around a three-tier data fetching strategy that ensures the site never serves a blank page:

  1. Primary: GraphQL queries via WPGraphQL — typed, structured content with a clean schema
  2. Secondary: WordPress REST API — triggered automatically when GraphQL is unavailable
  3. Tertiary: Static fallback data bundled with the build — keeps the site up even during complete CMS outages

A SectionRenderer → SectionFactory → SectionTransformers pipeline maps CMS data directly to reusable UI sections. Homepage layout changes become a CMS operation, not a code deployment.

Performance & Infrastructure

  • Next.js Image component with automatic WebP/AVIF conversion and blur placeholders
  • Dynamic imports to keep the initial JavaScript bundle lean
  • Standalone output mode for a minimal production deployment footprint
  • PM2 process management with a custom Node.js server for zero-downtime restarts
  • Git-based deployment to Plesk VPS via deploy.sh, with WordPress CMS hosted separately on Hostinger

A Real Debugging Story

One subtle issue encountered during development: an ACF field group that was correctly registered and matched by location rules, but Gutenberg’s meta box panel was hidden for the logged-in user. The root cause was Gutenberg storing per-user hidden panel preferences — the group existed but was invisible in the editor. The fix was rendering the affected fields as a dedicated metabox to bypass the panel preference system entirely. The kind of bug you only find by actually relying on the CMS day-to-day.

key_features.title: Three-Tier Data Fallback
key_features.description: GraphQL → REST API → static bundle. The site serves content even when WordPress is completely unreachable.

key_features.title: ACF as Code
key_features.description: All field groups are defined in PHP and versioned in git — identical schema across local, staging, and production with no migration drift.

key_features.title: Configurable CORS & Redirects
key_features.description: Origins and redirect behavior are wp-admin configurable with production PHP constants that override settings for security.

key_features.title: Headless Admin Dashboard
key_features.description: Custom wp-admin panel with REST/GraphQL health checks, CORS diagnostics, and copy-paste curl commands for rapid debugging.

key_features.title: Dynamic Section Architecture
key_features.description: Homepage layout is driven by CMS data via a SectionRenderer pipeline — content and structure changes without code deployments.

key_features.title: Decoupled Infrastructure
key_features.description: Frontend (Next.js on Plesk VPS) and CMS (WordPress on Hostinger) are independently hosted, scaled, and deployed.

Vorarlberger Landeskrankenhäuser Digital Transformation
A comprehensive digital annual report platform for Austria’s Vorarlberger Landeskrankenhäuser, serving 537,900+ patient contacts annually across 5 hospital locations with 4,710+ employees.

project_content.challenge:
The Vorarlberger Landeskrankenhäuser needed to transform their traditional printed annual report into a dynamic, engaging digital experience that would:

Business Challenges:

  • Present complex healthcare data (43,700 operations, 77,900 inpatient treatments, 2,800 births annually) in an accessible format
  • Showcase their “Arbeiten mit Herz” (Working with Heart) philosophy across 5 hospital locations
  • Provide stakeholder transparency for one of Austria’s largest healthcare networks
  • Create a platform for ongoing communication beyond the annual report cycle

Technical Challenges:

  • Handle large volumes of statistical data and interactive visualizations
  • Ensure fast loading times despite rich multimedia content (video statements, interactive maps)
  • Create a scalable CMS for multiple departments and locations
  • Implement robust SEO for healthcare-related content in German markets
project_content.solution:
🏥 Digital Healthcare Storytelling Platform

Built a comprehensive WordPress solution that transforms dry statistics into compelling human stories:

Key Features Developed:

  • Interactive Statistics Dashboard: Real-time display of 537,900 patient contacts, 43,700 operations, and other key metrics
  • Leadership Statement System: Custom post types for executive interviews and department head statements
  • Location-Based Content: Dynamic content for 5 hospital locations (Feldkirch, Bregenz, Hohenems, Rankweil, Bludenz)
  • Employee Spotlight Platform: “Mein Job fürs Leben” (My Job for Life) recruitment integration
  • Department Management System: Custom taxonomy for 7 management areas including IT, Finance, and Quality Management

project_content.challenge: The campaign needed a site that could showcase multimedia content prominently, rather than fitting that content into a general-purpose corporate template. The target audience — prospective apprentices — required a different tone and structure than the main illwerke vkw brand site.
project_content.solution:
I developed a custom WordPress theme meticulously tailored to the specific needs and visual identity of the ‘Your Energy’ campaign. The website was engineered to be lightweight, easily manageable, and highly optimized for performance across all devices. A key focus was the seamless integration of diverse multimedia elements, including campaign videos and social media content, designed to serve as “eye-catchers” (such as dance moves) and to illustrate real-life scenes from the training routine. Navigation was kept simple and intuitive, ensuring an engaging and user-friendly experience for a younger target group, and effectively demonstrating how illwerke vkw empowers young people to co-create the energy future.

Key Features:

  • Custom WordPress Theme: Built to fit campaign visuals and brand guidelines, supporting the ‘Your Energy’ aesthetic.

  • Multimedia Integration: Smooth embedding of videos, animations, and social media content, incorporating campaign-specific visual elements like dance moves and training scenes.

  • Optimized Performance: Fast loading and responsive across all devices, ensuring a seamless experience for a mobile-first generation.

  • User-Friendly CMS: Flexible backend for editors to update content quickly during the campaign, allowing for agile content management.

excerpt.rendered: A WordPress plugin for managing a freelance career from wp-admin, integrating AI-powered research, proposals, and GitHub portfolio sync.

project_content.challenge: Freelancers working from WordPress need a unified place to research prospective clients, draft proposals, track outcomes, store documents, and keep their portfolio in sync with their GitHub activity. Without tooling, these tasks are spread across separate apps and manual workflows. This plugin consolidates all of that into a single wp-admin interface.
project_content.solution: Work OS is built as a PHP WordPress plugin that creates four custom database tables and a set of admin screens for each workflow area. It integrates Anthropic Claude Sonnet for proposal drafting, fit analysis, and blog generation, and Google Gemini 2.5 Flash with Search grounding for company research. GitHub Sync compares public repos against a custom post type for portfolio projects, reads actual README content via the GitHub API, and uses Claude to populate ACF fields on generated draft CPT entries. All API calls are manually triggered; no background processing runs automatically.

key_features.title: AI-Powered Company Research and Fit Analysis
key_features.description: Paste a job description to trigger a Gemini search-grounded company brief, then run a Claude fit analysis that scores your profile against your CV, GitHub activity, and memory log entries.

key_features.title: Proposal Drafting and Archive
key_features.description: Log proposals with company, budget, status, and notes. Claude generates a pitch letter from the research context, stored alongside the fit analysis and expandable inline in the archive view.

key_features.title: GitHub Portfolio Sync
key_features.description: Compares public GitHub repos against portfolio project CPTs, identifies repos with substantial READMEs missing from the portfolio, and uses Claude to generate fully populated ACF draft entries flagged for review before publishing.

key_features.title: Memory Log and Blog Generation
key_features.description: Freeform memory entries about contacts, decisions, and learnings are stored and referenced by Claude during fit analysis. Blog posts can be generated from a chosen topic and optionally anchored to a memory event, then published as WordPress drafts in one click.

excerpt.rendered: A WordPress plugin for accounting firms to manage client companies, employees, and terminations with PDF generation and email delivery.

project_content.challenge: Accounting firms need a structured way to manage multiple client companies and their employees within a single WordPress instance, while ensuring each client can only access their own data. Additionally, the workflow requires generating and distributing formal employee data sheets and termination notices as PDFs.
project_content.solution: Built as a WordPress plugin using custom post types for employees and terminations, with a custom role (kunden_v2) enforcing data isolation per client. DomPDF handles PDF generation for both employee profiles and termination notices, with AJAX endpoints enabling in-browser PDF preview, download, and direct email delivery to clients and bookkeeping addresses. All data is stored in standard WordPress posts and meta, requiring no custom database tables.

key_features.title: Role-Based Client Isolation
key_features.description: A custom kunden_v2 WordPress role restricts each client account to viewing and managing only their own employees, enforced via ownership filtering on the employee custom post type.

key_features.title: PDF Generation for Employees
key_features.description: Generates formatted employee data sheets as PDFs via DomPDF, with configurable header, footer, and company address pulled from plugin settings stored in wp_options.

key_features.title: Termination Notice Workflow
key_features.description: Supports creating termination records (Kündigung) linked to employee posts, with dedicated PDF generation and AJAX-triggered email delivery for termination notices.

key_features.title: Austrian-Specific Employee Profiles
key_features.description: Employee records include Austria-specific fields such as Sozialversicherungsnummer, Staatsangehörigkeit, and Dienstverhältnisart, reflecting local legal and HR requirements.

excerpt.rendered: A WordPress plugin providing an Elementor-based VIP booking flow for Oktoberfest events, including search, booking form, and admin configuration.

project_content.challenge: Event organizers needed a structured VIP booking experience integrated directly into WordPress and Elementor, with configurable availability date ranges and page routing. A custom solution was required rather than a generic booking plugin to support the specific Oktoberfest VIP workflow.
project_content.solution: Built as a self-contained WordPress plugin that registers two custom Elementor widgets: a VIP search widget and a booking form widget. Admin settings allow configuration of date ranges per year, page mapping for booking and confirmation pages, and optional API integration. Assets are compiled via a Node.js/npm build pipeline using Sass.

excerpt.rendered: A local Flask web app that converts uploaded audio files to text using OpenAI Whisper, running fully offline after setup.

project_content.challenge: Transcribing audio content such as voice notes, meetings, and lectures typically requires sending data to external services, raising privacy concerns. This project addresses the need for an accurate, offline-capable transcription tool that keeps all audio and text data on the user’s own machine.
project_content.solution: The app is built with Flask as the web server and uses OpenAI Whisper for local speech-to-text transcription, with FFmpeg handling audio format processing. Users upload audio files through a browser interface, select a Whisper model size, and receive transcriptions stored in a local SQLite database. Audio files are deleted after processing, and transcription history can be exported as plain text, JSON, or SRT subtitle files.

key_features.title: Offline-First Transcription
key_features.description: After the initial model download, all processing runs locally with no data sent to external servers. Audio files are automatically deleted after transcription.

key_features.title: Multiple Whisper Model Options
key_features.description: Users can choose from five Whisper model sizes (tiny, base, small, medium, large) to balance transcription speed against accuracy depending on their hardware.

key_features.title: Multi-Format Audio Support
key_features.description: Accepts .opus, .m4a, .ogg, .mp3, and .wav files up to 25MB each, with support for processing multiple files at once via drag-and-drop or file browse.

key_features.title: Flexible Export Options
key_features.description: Transcription history is saved locally in SQLite and can be exported in plain text, JSON, or SRT subtitle format.

project_content.challenge: The old website was outdated, poorly structured, and not mobile-friendly. The goal was to build a new digital presence that conveys professionalism and trust, clearly presents the range of services, and makes it easy for patients to book appointments. It also needed to be easy to maintain internally and optimized for local visibility.
project_content.solution: I rebuilt the entire site using WordPress and restructured the content around the practice’s goals. The new design is clean, modern, and patient-oriented. All service and content pages were optimized for search engines to boost local rankings. The CMS was configured to allow the team to update content independently. The build focused on performance, SEO fundamentals, and a solid technical base.

project_content.challenge: The old site was outdated, hard to maintain, and didn’t reflect the brand’s current positioning. The goal was to rebuild the website with a fresh, modern design, improve performance and SEO, and give the client full control over content updates via a flexible CMS.
project_content.solution: I developed a fully custom WordPress theme with Gutenberg and ACF blocks to match the new design system and content structure. I handled the entire build, including performance optimization, SEO setup, and content implementation. GSAP was used for subtle motion effects, and the setup was optimized for speed using Cloudflare CDN, Nginx, and modern caching strategies. The result is a clean, performant site that’s easy for the client to manage and scale.

project_content.challenge: The existing website was outdated and no longer reflected Martina’s minimalist interior design aesthetic. The goal was to rebuild it based on a new Figma design while ensuring high performance, clean structure, and a CMS setup that allowed Martina to manage and add content independently. SEO was a key focus from the start to improve visibility and organic reach.
project_content.solution: I developed a new site using a custom Elementor workflow tailored to her content structure and technical needs. A custom child theme was implemented with specific functions to keep the build lean and performant. WP Rocket and Imagify were configured for caching and image optimization, while Rank Math handled structured SEO. The result is a clean, responsive site that’s easy for the client to maintain without sacrificing performance or flexibility.
